_article: XXXIX.7 — 'Distant/medium/near render fidelity per region.' User directive: 'A hill's exact height (96 vs 101 blocks) is the adapter's business, governed by the LOD region's fidelity.'
_description: Schema for the COMPILED LOD (Level of Detail) Regions — the spatial partition that tells the Minecraft adapter how faithfully to render each region. Distant regions render as low-poly silhouettes (a mountain is a triangle, a city is a block). Near regions render at full block resolution. The simulation does not care about LOD; this exists to make rendering tractable at world scale.

fidelity_rules.terrain_resolution: enum — silhouette | low_poly | medium | full_block — how terrain is approximated
fidelity_rules.structure_resolution: enum — marker | footprint | outline | full — how settlements/sects render
fidelity_rules.entity_render_distance: int — how far NPCs/beasts are visible
fidelity_rules.momentum_visibility: enum — none | major_thresholds | perceivable_cues | full — which momentum perceivable cues render at this LOD
fidelity_rules.spirit_render: enum — none | ambient | particles | full — how spirit density is visualized

lod_regions.transition_to: string — the next-finer LOD region id (for smooth transitions as the player approaches)
lod_regions.canonical_focus: bool — true for regions the player is expected to spend time in (Chapter 1 locations) — these default to 'full' fidelity

default_fidelity_by_distance.medium_threshold_blocks: int — beyond this (but within distant), medium
default_fidelity_by_distance.near_threshold_blocks: int — beyond this (but within medium), near

queries_supported: canonical_focus_regions() -> [region_id] — regions that must always render at full fidelity (Chapter 1)

_compile_source: World Blueprint DSL v2 — canonical_focus designation per region + distance-from-player-origin defaults
_runtime_consumers: ChunkRenderer (Minecraft adapter) — selects terrain/structure/entity/spirit render fidelity per chunk based on its LOD region
